description: Cascade-based algorithm: Code Reality > Live State > Documentation (evidence_grade). Replaces old authoritative_source → temporal_conflict → user_review flow.

steps: Classify fact type: objective (math, code, physics) or subjective/ambiguous (opinions, historical claims without proof)
If objective → apply cascade priority. User override is REJECTED
If subjective → user override is ALLOWED (final say to user)
Apply cascade: priority 1 (code) > priority 2 (live) > priority 3 (docs with evidence_grade)
Example: GitHub issue (live_state, priority 2) > docs (documentation, priority 3)

decision_rules_integration.dr-1: Script reports raw overlap only — do not change scores automatically
decision_rules_integration.dr-2: If B contains additions/deletions (correction) + evidence → evaluate correction, B > A on corrected claim
decision_rules_integration.dr-3: Attribution conflict → A wins original; B only reporter

type_classification.rule: Classify each source as documentation | code_reality | live_state (reality layer)

categories.description: Machine-verifiable: code compiles, tests pass, API responses
Ephemeral but observable: current API status, server metrics, runtime state
Authoritative docs: blogs, manuals, articles — lowest priority

cascade_order.description: Code compiles or not — deterministic, machine-verifiable. Always wins.
cascade_order.example: GitHub issue shows infinite loop > docs say API works

cascade_order.description: API returns X right now — ephemeral but observable. Wins over documentation.
Authoritative docs win only when no code_reality or live_state available.
cascade_order.note: This is the ONLY layer where domain authority matters

evidence_grade_sub_priority.description: Fact from authoritative source (official docs, project wiki, core maintainer blog)
Claim without confirmation or from weak source (generic blog, forum post)

resolution_priority.condition: source_layer == code && source exists in repository
agent_confidence < threshold OR conflicting cascade priorities

fallback_chain.action: human_override → flag in answer with CONFLICT tag

arbitration_layer.description: Arbitration layer: objective facts vs subjective claims. User override only for subjective.

fact_types.rule: If fact is objective (math, code, empirical facts) → user cannot override. Agent gently recommends verification.
If fact is subjective/ambiguous → user has final say (highest authority). Agent applies user decision.

resolution_logic.step_1: classify conflict as objective or subjective
resolution_logic.step_2_if_objective: apply cascade priority. User override → REJECTED with gentle recommendation
resolution_logic.step_3_if_subjective: user_override_accepted → apply user decision to wiki

logging_actions.format: ## [date] query | Question -> detected X contradictions, Y comparisons
logging_actions.trigger: after contradiction detection, before answering user

detection_output.rule: If contradictions found → add '## ⚠ Contradictions' section to answer before main content
